mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 50% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 65% of high-inflation months without subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects aggregate economic growth and rising corporate profit margins, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero correlation with 60-day equity returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruptions, which reduces earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances following a 1.0% monthly industrial production drop.
